publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-01-21 23:59:58 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Research material including Moog research, Dept. of Science and Technologies Studies records, course and teaching material, correspondence, talks and lectures, articles and publications

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Trevor Pinch was born in Lisnaskea, Northern Ireland on 1 January 1952. He was Distinguished Professor of Arts and Sciences in the Department of Science and Technology Studies in the College of Arts and Sciences at Cornell University. His areas of research included the sociology of technology and how users engage with technology, sound studies and music and in particular the development of musical instruments and sound objects, markets and the economy with specific attention to the study of selling and persuasion. Trevor was also part of the Electric Golem music group with James Spitznagel.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Trevor Pinch papers, #14-37-4433. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions
accessrestrict.p: Personal home videos and personal digital material on CDs or floppy disks are restricted to the donor.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Robert Moog papers, #8629. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Music and technology.
Sound studies
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Science & Technology Studies

did.unittitle: Series I. Talks, lectures, and other events

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged loosely in chronological order by Pinch.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: For Moog talks and lectures see Sub-subseries IIe.1. Moog/Analog Days.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Bath lectures and job talks from the 1990s.
